summary: Quarantine records with null required fields to _rejected/ prefix instead of failing job
rationale: The original job raised a RuntimeError when records contained nulls on required columns, causing a hard failure. The patch implements the catalog's recommended remediation (option a): it identifies records with nulls on any required column, writes them to a quarantine prefix at s3://<silver-bucket>/_rejected/transactions/reason=null_required_fields/, and continues processing valid records. This preserves data lineage, allows the job to succeed with partial data, and enables human review of rejected records. The quarantine logic constructs a filter condition that checks all REQUIRED_NON_NULL_COLUMNS and splits the dataframe before any downstream transformations.
